formatted_summary:
agent2 (Round 1 summarized): Brachycephaly results from early fusion of specific cranial sutures; specifically, it involves the sagittal suture which runs longitudinally across the top of the skull, leading to an abnormally shortened and broadened head form when fused prematurely. \boxed{Sagittal suture}
To summarize Agent2’s reasoning into two concise sentences:

Brachycephaly occurs due to premature fusion of particular cranial sutures, most notably involving the sagittal suture that extends lengthwise over the crown of the head. This fusion limits growth in one direction, causing a characteristically short and wide head appearance. 

This summary captures both critical aspects—specifically mentioning "premature fusion" as required for brachycephaly formation—and identifying the primary involved structure ("the sagittal suture"). It also conveys how this affects morphology accurately within brief statements aligned with provided constraints about focus areas like anatomy and resultant physical characteristics seen clinically. 
\boxed{\text{Premature fusion of cranial sutures causes brachycephaly, particularly affecting the sagittal suture leading to a short, wide head shape}}. Answer choice: B
